## What's changed

Focus on PostgreSQL connection reliability and clearer refresh-button messaging.

### Added
- The header's refresh button now shows a tooltip explaining what it actually does (reload the current snapshot, not trigger a new Alertmanager poll) and displays the backend's poll interval, exposed via a new `poll_interval_seconds` field on `GET /api/v1/status`.

### Fixed
- Capped the PostgreSQL connection pool (`JARVIS_DB_MAX_OPEN_CONNS`, default 10) instead of leaving it unbounded. An unbounded pool let request bursts open as many connections as there were parallel queries across all pods, exhausting a shared RDS instance's connection slots (`SQLSTATE 53300`) and causing stats/heatmap requests to fail with 500s. The Helm chart now exposes this as `database.maxOpenConns`.

**Full diff:** [v1.9.0...v1.9.1](https://github.com/kj187/jarvis/compare/v1.9.0...v1.9.1)
